summary: The test suite is solid and covers all four primary functions (Get-RenamePreview, Find-RenameConflicts, Invoke-FileRename, New-UndoScript) with matching Describe blocks. All three headline requirements — preview mode, undo capability, and conflict detection — are exercised, including an integration test that actually executes the generated undo script. Edge cases are meaningful: empty directory, no-op matches, spaces in filenames, single-quote escaping, multiple capture groups, and missing source paths. Design is clean: BeforeEach/AfterEach with GUID-based temp dirs ensures test isolation, $script: scoping is used correctly, and test names are descriptive. Gaps keeping this from a 5: FullOldPath/FullNewPath properties on plan objects are never asserted (only OldName/NewName are checked); no test verifies Invoke-FileRename does not partially rename files when a mid-run failure occurs; conflict-with-existing-file path is not exercised end-to-end through Invoke-FileRename (only through Find-RenameConflicts directly); no test for passing a file path instead of a directory to Get-RenamePreview; and the error message text for the conflict abort in Invoke-FileRename uses a loose '*conflict*' wildcard that could miss regressions. Overall a trustworthy suite that would catch the vast majority of regressions.
